title: elymus interruptus
intro: Elymus interruptus, also known as wildrye or tall wheatgrass, is a perennial grass native to the United States and Canada. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Elymus interruptus include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for various health conditions. Elymus interruptus can be prepared in various ways, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
